userMessage: create an eye diagram on meas 3

response.text: Done — The scope shows DPOJET eye/jitter analysis on Ref1 with an open but deterministic-jitter-limited eye at about 2.5008 Gb/s. Visible plots are Eye Diagram (Meas 1), TIE Spectrum (Meas 1), TIE Histogram (Meas 1), Bathtub (Meas 1), and the waveform view. The eye overlay has a midlevel near 0.080 mV, eye crossings around the center, and a visibly narrowed horizontal opening. The jitter table lists total jitter about 54.07 ps and random jitter about 1.68 ps, indicating deterministic jitter dominates. The right-side measurement badges show Meas 3 Frequency statistics enabled with mean 1.106 GHz, sigma 298.4 MHz, max 1.266 GHz, min 359.0 MHz, N=1874; Meas 4 Amplitude 744.8 mV; Meas 5 Peak-to-Peak 1.104 V; Meas 6 Rise Time 145.4 ps; Meas 7 Fall Time 220.4 ps; and a new Meas 8 Frequency at 1.106 GHz. I do not see a new eye-diagram plot for Meas 3 on this captured screen; the displayed eye plot is still labeled “Plot 1 - Eye Diagram (Meas 1)”.
